articles.title: Embed Integrates with Square POS, Connecting F&B and Cashless Card Systems for FECs
articles.summary: Embed has launched a direct integration with Square POS, allowing FEC operators to sell Embed card products through the Square platform and unify retail, F&B, and cashless game card transactions in a single ecosystem. The move eliminates the need for multiple disconnected systems and reduces friction at the point of sale. This is a significant operational upgrade for smaller and mid-size FECs already running Square who want to add or expand cashless arcade and attraction technology.

articles.title: Accesso Appoints Lee Cowie as CEO, Accelerates AI Strategy
articles.summary: Accesso Technology Group, which provides ticketing, queuing, and venue management technology to over 1,100 leisure and entertainment venues worldwide, has named Lee Cowie as its new chief executive officer following a planned leadership transition. Cowie, a former accesso client who joined as COO 18 months ago, is expected to push the company's expanding data intelligence and AI capabilities. FEC operators who rely on accesso's platform should watch for strategic shifts in product direction under new leadership.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park to Pay $300K Settlement After Go-Kart Death of 6-Year-Old
articles.summary: An Urban Air location in Port St. Lucie, Florida has agreed to a $300,000 settlement following an investigation into a go-kart crash that killed a six-year-old child. The case highlights significant liability risks associated with go-kart attractions at FECs, particularly regarding safety protocols and equipment standards. FEC operators with go-kart attractions should review safety procedures, rider height and age requirements, and insurance coverage in light of this case.

articles.title: Florida Settles with Family of Child Killed in Go-Kart Crash at FEC
articles.summary: A settlement has been reached between the state of Florida and the family of a six-year-old who died in a go-kart crash at an indoor entertainment center, underscoring the serious safety and legal consequences that can follow ride incidents. The $300,000 agreement draws attention to regulatory oversight of FEC attractions and potential gaps in safety enforcement. Operators across the country should treat this as a reminder to audit ride safety policies and staff training rigorously.

articles.title: Intercard Hires Bowling Industry Veteran JR Huyck as North American Sales Manager
articles.summary: Intercard, a leading provider of cashless technology for FECs and bowling centers, has brought on JR Huyck as its new North American Sales Manager. Huyck brings deep roots in the bowling industry, having grown up in the business and developed relationships across the sector. The hire signals Intercard's continued focus on expanding its footprint in bowling and family entertainment venues across North America.

articles.title: Five Intercard Operator Customers Win Bowling Center Architecture & Design Awards
articles.summary: Five venues using Intercard's cashless systems were recognized at the 41st annual Bowling Center Architecture and Design Awards, hosted by Bowling Center Management magazine. The recognition highlights how leading FEC operators are investing in facility design alongside technology upgrades. For operators considering a remodel, the award-winning venues serve as a benchmark for best-in-class facility presentation.

articles.title: Six Flags Brings Back Holiday in the Park at Two Locations with Full Redesign
articles.summary: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation is relaunching Holiday in the Park at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey and Six Flags Over Georgia in 2026, with a completely reimagined seasonal event built from the ground up. The revamped holiday programming emphasizes immersive experiences and is positioned as a major draw for the winter season. FEC and attraction operators can take note of the trend toward fully redesigned seasonal events rather than incremental updates.

articles.title: Family Kingdom's New Raptor Roller Coaster Delayed by Permitting Challenges
articles.summary: Family Kingdom Amusement Park in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina has pushed back the opening of its highly anticipated Raptor single-rail steel roller coaster due to permitting and construction hurdles. Owner Corky Powers confirmed the ride will not open until later in the year at the earliest. The delay is a reminder to FEC and park operators of the permitting complexities that can affect attraction installation timelines.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Named One of USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has earned recognition from USA Today as one of the most trusted brands among parents, a significant credibility marker for the family entertainment category. The designation reinforces the brand's ongoing repositioning efforts following its post-bankruptcy relaunch. For competing FEC operators, this highlights the importance of brand trust and safety perception in attracting family audiences.

articles.title: Accesso Names Lee Cowie CEO in Planned Leadership Transition
articles.summary: Accesso Technology Group has promoted chief operating officer Lee Cowie to CEO, succeeding Steve Brown who founded the accesso business in 2008. The company serves more than 1,100 venues in 31 countries with ticketing, POS, and operational technology. Cowie's background as a former accesso client is expected to bring an operator-focused perspective to the company's next growth phase.

articles.title: Breeze Creative Delivers Custom Interactive Tech for Bluey's House Touring Experience
articles.summary: Breeze Creative has partnered with BeFun to supply bespoke interactive technology for Bluey's House, a touring immersive location-based entertainment experience based on the hit children's series. The installation integrates physical set design with digital interactives to drive storytelling, active play, and educational outcomes for families. FEC operators interested in IP-driven LBE attractions can look to this project as a model for scalable, narrative-led technology integration.

articles.title: Why Most Immersive Experience Projects Never Get Built — and How to Avoid That Fate
articles.summary: A new analysis explores the common but rarely discussed reality that most immersive and experiential entertainment projects stall before construction ever begins, typically due to a slow buildup of misaligned expectations rather than a single failure point. The piece offers practical insight for FEC developers and investors navigating the gap between concept enthusiasm and project execution. Operators planning new attractions or venue expansions will find the discussion of development friction directly applicable.

articles.title: Three Ways Dark Ride Operators Can Elevate Guest Experiences with Modern Technology
articles.summary: As new benchmark attractions like Universal's Epic Universe raise guest expectations for dark rides, a new guide outlines three practical strategies for delivering compelling experiences, including the shift from pre-rendered to real-time content. The piece is relevant for FEC and attraction operators considering investment in immersive ride experiences or interactive dark ride upgrades. Real-time content generation in particular opens new possibilities for personalization and repeatability.

articles.title: New Kidtopia Indoor Playground with Obstacle Courses and Climbing Coming to Dublin
articles.summary: Kidtopia, a new indoor playground concept featuring obstacle courses, indoor climbing, and active play areas, is set to open in Dublin. The venue represents continued growth in the soft-play and active family entertainment segment. FEC operators can monitor Kidtopia as an emerging competitor or concept in the indoor children's entertainment space.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline Park Opens High-Energy Family Venue at Rivergate
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has opened a new high-energy family entertainment venue at Rivergate, expanding its trampoline park and active entertainment footprint. The opening reflects continued demand for active, family-oriented indoor attractions. FEC operators can look to the venue's programming and format as a reference point for the competitive trampoline park segment.

articles.title: Ellis & Associates Recognizes 2026 Elite Aquatic Safety Professionals
articles.summary: Ellis & Associates, the leading global aquatic safety and risk management firm, has announced its 2026 Associate and Senior Associate honorees, recognizing more than 100 elite lifeguard instructor trainers for achievement and dedication. For FEC operators with water play areas, splash pads, or waterpark elements, maintaining certified aquatic safety staff is a critical liability management priority. This recognition program sets a professional benchmark for aquatic safety training standards.

articles.title: Rage Room Concept 'Breaking Point' Set to Open in Kitimat, BC
articles.summary: A new rage room attraction called Breaking Point is preparing to open in Kitimat, British Columbia, adding to the growing list of alternative entertainment concepts targeting stress-relief experiences. Rage rooms have gained traction as a low-barrier FEC add-on or standalone attraction, particularly for adult audiences. Operators exploring experiential entertainment beyond traditional games and rides may find the concept worth watching.

articles.title: ISmash Smash Room Experience Opening in Rapid City, South Dakota
articles.summary: ISmash, a smash room and rage room concept, is opening a new location in Rapid City, South Dakota in May. The concept allows guests to safely break and smash objects as a cathartic experience, representing a growing niche in experiential indoor entertainment. FEC operators in smaller markets should note that smash room attractions are proving viable even outside major metro areas.

articles.title: Amoeba Bengaluru Relaunches as Next-Gen Sports Bar with Entertainment Focus
articles.summary: Amoeba, a well-known entertainment venue in Bengaluru, India, has relaunched with a next-generation sports bar concept, blending dining, entertainment, and sports viewing into a single experience. The repositioning reflects a broader global trend of entertainment centers evolving into hybrid food-and-fun destinations. FEC operators considering a F&B and entertainment integration strategy may find the Amoeba relaunch a useful international case study.

articles.title: MSC World Asia Cruise Ship Unveils FEC-Style Entertainment Including Bumper Cars and Game Shows
articles.summary: MSC Cruises has revealed the full entertainment lineup for MSC World Asia, including bumper cars, roller skating, a ropes course, a dry slide, and original interactive game shows in a dedicated family entertainment arena. The ship's Clubhouse and Luna Park Arena concepts mirror land-based FEC formats applied at sea, offering design and programming inspiration for FEC operators. The attraction mix demonstrates how cruise lines are borrowing heavily from the FEC playbook to drive family engagement.

articles.title: Amusement Center Agrees to $300K Settlement Following Child Death Investigation
articles.summary: An amusement center has agreed to pay $300,000 following a regulatory investigation into the death of a young girl at the facility. The case serves as a serious reminder for FEC operators of the safety, liability, and reputational risks associated with operating physical attractions. Operators should use high-profile incidents like this to prompt internal safety audits and ensure compliance with all applicable regulations.

articles.title: Laredo Gambling Raid at Amusement Center Results in 6 Arrests and 88 Machine Seizures
articles.summary: A law enforcement raid on an amusement center in Laredo, Texas resulted in six arrests and the seizure of 88 gaming machines suspected of illegal gambling activity. The incident is a cautionary tale for FEC operators regarding the legal boundaries of prize redemption and gaming machine operations. Operators should ensure their game and redemption offerings are clearly compliant with state and local gaming regulations to avoid similar enforcement actions.

articles.title: Icon Park Implements Chaperone Policy After Teen Takeover Incidents and Arrests
articles.summary: Icon Park in Orlando has introduced a chaperone policy requiring adults to accompany young visitors following a series of disruptive teen takeover events and arrests at the venue. The policy change reflects a growing challenge for entertainment venues managing large groups of unsupervised youth. FEC operators should evaluate their own guest conduct policies and consider whether similar chaperone or supervision requirements could protect staff, guests, and venue reputation.

articles.title: Creative Studio Berlin Wins Thea Award for Immersive Educational Experience
articles.summary: Creative Studio Berlin has received a TEA Thea Award in the Historical Experience category for The Secret Laboratory, a 50-minute immersive educational experience set in Renaissance-era Saxony that blends storytelling, hands-on learning, and themed environments. The project demonstrates the growing appeal of edutainment formats that combine escape room-style engagement with historical content for families and school groups. FEC operators with escape room offerings or event programming may find the format a compelling model for differentiated experiences.

articles.title: Large Illinois Indoor Amusement Center Spotlighted as a Family Destination
articles.summary: A feature highlights a large indoor amusement center in Illinois as a standout family entertainment destination, drawing attention to the continued appeal of sprawling, multi-attraction indoor venues. The coverage reinforces that well-executed large-format FECs remain competitive in today's entertainment landscape. Operators can use such profiles to benchmark their own attraction mix and guest experience positioning.
